circuit-sat.name: Circuit-SAT
circuit-sat.text: Does a boolean circuit have a satisfying assignment?

cnf-sat.name: CNF-SAT
cnf-sat.text: Does a boolean formula in conjunctive normal form have a satisfying assignment?

3-sat.name: 3-CNF-SAT
3-sat.text: Does a boolean formula in conjunctive normal form, where each clause has exactly 3 literals, have a satisfying assignment?

indset.name: Independent Set
indset.text: Is there a subset of size at least k of vertices of an undirected graph such that no 2 vertices in the subset have an edge between them in the graph?

vertex-cover.name: Vertex Cover
vertex-cover.text: Is there a subset of size at most k of vertices of an undirected graph such that every edge has an endpoint in the subset?

clique.name: Clique
clique.text: Does a graph have a complete subgraph of size k?

set-cover.name: Set Cover
set-cover.text: Given a set U and some of its subsets, are there k subsets whose union is U?

ham-cycle.name: Hamiltonian Cycle
ham-cycle.text: Given an undirected graph on n vertices, does it have a cycle of length n?

tsp.name: Travelling Salesman Problem
tsp.text: Given a weighted complete graph, is there a hamiltonian cycle of weight at most w?

subset-sum.name: Subset Sum
subset-sum.text: Given a set S of integers and an integer k, does a subset of S sum to k?

ilp.name: Integer Linear Programming
ilp.text: Solving a linear program where the variables can only take integral values.

partition.name: Set Partitioning
partition.text: Given set of numbers, partition it into 2 subsets such that they both have the same sum.

best-partition.name: Best Set Partitioning
best-partition.text: Given set of numbers, partition it into 2 subsets such that the difference between their sums is at most d.

ham-path.name: Hamiltonian Path
ham-path.text: Given an undirected graph on n vertices, does it have a simple path of length n-1?

knapsack.name: Knapsack
knapsack.text: Given n items, each with a certain profit and size, and given a knapsack of limited capacity, can we pack a subset of items to get a profit ≥ P?

3-color.name: Graph 3-coloring
3-color.text: Given a graph, is it possible to color each vertex with one of 3 colors so that no 2 adjacent vertices have the same color?

d-ham-path.name: Directed Hamiltonian Path
d-ham-path.text: Given a directed graph on n vertices, does it have a path of length n-1?

d-ham-cycle.name: Directed Hamiltonian Cycle
d-ham-cycle.text: Given a directed graph on n vertices, does it have a cycle of length n?

makespan.name: Makespan scheduling
makespan.text: Given n jobs to run on k processors and the time required for each job, is it possible to finish all jobs in time ≤ T?

bin-packing.name: Bin Packing
bin-packing.text: Given the size of n items, can they be packed in k bins of capacity C?

max-cut.name: Max cut
max-cut.text: Given a graph G = (V, E), find a partitioning (S, V-S) such that E∩(S×(V-S)) is maximized.

edges.text: Every CNF formula has an equivalent depth-2 circuit.
CLRS ed3, Theorem 34.9, page 1080.

Algorithm Design, Kleinberg-Tardos, Theorem 8.8, page 460. (For every clause k, create i vertices if there are i variables in the clause. Draw an edge between all vertices of a clause and an edge between complementary vertices across clauses.)
A 3-CNF-SAT instance is also a CNF-SAT instance.
In a graph (V, E), S is a vertex cover iff V-S is an independent set.
S is an independent set in graph G iff S is a clique in the complement of G.
For each vertex v, create the set Sv of all edges incident on v. If some of these sets span the set of edges, then the corresponding vertices form a vertex cover.
Given a graph G, construct a weighted graph G' where c(u, v) = 0 if (u, v) in G and c(u, v) = 1 otherwise. Then G' has a 0-weight tour iff G has a hamiltonian cycle.
CLRS ed3, Theorem 34.15, page 1097.
Represent the set cover instance as a linear program. Each set corresponds to a boolean variable and the requirement of covering each element becomes a linear constraint.
CLRS ed3, Exercise 34.5-5, page 1101. See solutions by Bodnar and Lohr.
CLRS ed3, Exercise 34.5-6, page 1101. See solutions by Bodnar and Lohr.
For every number x in the set, create an item of weight x and size x. If the target is t, get a knapsack of size t. A subset sums to t iff we can get profit ≥ t.
Let s be the sum of set X. X can be partitioned into 2 subsets of equal sum iff there is a subset of X of sum s/2.
Add a constraint for each clause. For example, '(u1 or !u2 or u3)' becomes 'u1 + (1-u2) + u3 ≥ 1'.
CLRS ed3, Theorem 34.13, page 1091.
CLRS ed3, Problem 34.3, page 1103. See solutions by Bodnar and Lohr.
Computational Complexity (internet draft), Arora and Barak, Theorem 2.18, page 52.

Computational Complexity (internet draft), Arora and Barak, Theorem 2.18 (page 52) shows a polytime reduction from 3-sat to directed hamiltonian path. In the directed graph constructed there, add an edge from the end vertex to the start vertex.

Let X be a set of integers with sum s. For each integer x, create a job which requires x units of time. Then X can be partitioned into 2 equal-sum subsets iff all these jobs can be finished in time ≤ s/2 on 2 processors.
Let X be a set of integers with sum s. For each integer x, create an item of size x. Then X can be partitioned into 2 equal-sum subsets iff all these items can be packed in 2 bins.

For every vertex v in directed graph, create vertices v1, v2, v3 in undirected graph. For every edge (u, v) in directed graph, add edge (u3, v1) in undirected graph. Directed graph has ham-cycle iff undirected graph has ham-cycle.
Replace every undirected edge {u, v} by 2 directed edges (u, v) and (v, u). Directed graph has ham-cycle iff undirected graph has ham-cycle
CLRS ed3, Problem 34-2 d, page 1102. See solutions by Bodnar and Lohr.
A partition instance is a best-partition instance with difference 0.
